cards.back: the view that an agent's mental states depend on external factors such as the environment
the view that an agent's mental states depend only on internal factors such as the agent's own beliefs and desires
the ability of an agent to make decisions that maximize its chances of achieving its goals
the idea that an agent's mental states represent the environment in a way that is independent of the agent's actual experiences
the formal framework that describes how an agent perceives, processes, and reacts to its environment
